name: Svarozic
description: Svarozic, also known as Svarožič, is a Slavic deity primarily associated with fire, light, and blacksmithing. He is often considered to be a son of Svarog, the celestial blacksmith and creator god. Svarozic represents the earthly manifestation of fire, distinct from Svarog's celestial fire. He is a powerful and vital force, responsible for warmth, cooking, and the forging of tools and weapons. In some interpretations, he is associated with war and is depicted as a fierce warrior. Temples dedicated to Svarozic were often centers of fire worship, where eternal flames were maintained. He is seen as a protector and provider, ensuring the continuation of life and civilization through the power of fire. Svarozic's influence extended beyond the practical uses of fire, encompassing concepts of purification, transformation, and divine justice. He represents the active, dynamic force of creation and destruction inherent in fire. He is also sometimes associated with the sun, further emphasizing his connection to light and life. His worship was particularly prevalent among the Polabian Slavs, where he was held in high esteem as a powerful and benevolent god. Svarozic's significance lies in his embodiment of the essential element of fire and its multifaceted role in Slavic life and cosmology.
subtitle: God of Fire and Blacksmithing

keyMyths.title: Birth of Svarozic
keyMyths.description: Svarozic is born from the sparks of Svarog's divine forge, representing the earthly manifestation of fire.

keyMyths.title: Svarozic's Role in Blacksmithing
keyMyths.description: Svarozic oversees the creation of weapons and tools, empowering humanity through the art of blacksmithing.

keyMyths.title: Eternal Flame of Svarozic
keyMyths.description: Temples dedicated to Svarozic maintain an eternal flame, symbolizing his constant presence and power.
